unitInfo.book: The Old Woman Who Lived in a Shoe
unitInfo.author: Traditional

chapters.title: A Very Big Family
chapters.text:
Once upon a time, there lived an old woman who had so many children she didn't know what to do! She had twelve little ones, and they were always busy and loud.

The problem was that their house was much too small. It was so tiny that the children could barely fit inside. They were always bumping into each other and getting in the way.

The old woman loved her children very much, but she was tired and worried. How could she find a bigger place for her big family? Where could they all live together?

One day, while walking through the town, the old woman saw something very strange. There, in an empty field, sat the biggest shoe she had ever seen! It was as big as a house!

vocabulary.definition: only just; almost not at all
feeling troubled or anxious about something

comprehension.question: How many children did the old woman have?
What was the problem with their house?

informationalText.title: Plants
informationalText.content:
Plants are living things that make their own food using sunlight, water, and air. This process is called photosynthesis, and it happens in the green parts of plants, especially the leaves. Plants are very important because they produce the oxygen that we breathe and provide food for animals and humans. Without plants, there would be no life on Earth!
There are many different types of plants, from tiny grass to giant redwood trees. Some plants, like roses and daisies, grow beautiful flowers to attract insects that help them make seeds. Other plants, like pine trees, make cones instead of flowers. Plants can grow almost anywhere – in deserts, underwater, on mountains, and even in the cracks of sidewalks. They are amazing at adapting to different environments.

questions.question: How do plants make their own food?
options: through photosynthesis using sunlight, water, and air

questions.question: Why do some plants grow flowers?
options: to attract insects that help them make seeds

chapters.title: The Giant Shoe
chapters.text:
The old woman walked closer to the giant shoe. It was made of brown leather and had long laces just like a regular shoe. But it was enormous! It had windows and a door cut into it.

She knocked on the door. No one answered. She peeked through the windows. The shoe was empty inside! It had been turned into a house, but no one was living in it.

"This is perfect!" thought the old woman. "There's plenty of room for all of us here!" The shoe had a big main room, a kitchen, and even an upstairs area.

The old woman hurried home to tell her children the good news. "Pack up your things!" she called out. "We're moving to a new home! And wait until you see where we're going to live!"

comprehension.question: What was the giant shoe made of?
What did the old woman find when she looked inside the shoe?
options: It was empty with lots of room
It was full of old furniture

informationalText.title: Rocks
informationalText.content:
Rocks are solid materials made up of minerals that form naturally in the Earth. There are three main types of rocks: igneous rocks form when melted rock cools down, sedimentary rocks form from layers of sand and other materials pressed together over time, and metamorphic rocks form when existing rocks are changed by heat and pressure deep underground.
Rocks are useful to people in many ways. We use granite and marble for countertops and buildings, limestone to make cement, and coal for energy. Some rocks contain valuable metals like gold and silver. Geologists are scientists who study rocks to learn about Earth's history and find useful materials. By examining different rock layers, they can tell what the Earth was like millions of years ago.

questions.question: What are the three main types of rocks?
What do geologists do?
options: they study rocks to learn about Earth's history

chapters.title: Moving Day
chapters.text:
The children were so excited about their new home! They had never seen anything like a shoe house before. They packed up all their toys, clothes, and belongings as fast as they could.

When they arrived at the giant shoe, the children could not believe their eyes. "We're going to live in a shoe?" they laughed. "This is the best house ever!" they cheered.

They ran inside to explore their new home. There was so much space! The children could run around without bumping into each other. Each child could have their own special corner.

The old woman smiled as she watched her children explore. For the first time in a long while, she felt happy and peaceful. Her family finally had room to grow and play.

comprehension.question: How did the children feel about their new home?
What could each child have in the new house?

informationalText.title: Ocean
informationalText.content:
The ocean covers more than 70% of Earth's surface and contains about 97% of all the water on our planet. Ocean water is salty because it has dissolved minerals that have washed into it from rocks and soil over millions of years. The ocean is divided into five main parts: the Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Arctic, and Southern oceans, but they are all connected as one giant body of water.
The ocean is home to millions of different plants and animals, from tiny plankton to massive blue whales. It provides food for billions of people around the world and helps control Earth's weather and temperature. Ocean currents act like rivers in the sea, carrying warm and cold water around the planet. The ocean also gives us oxygen to breathe – tiny ocean plants called phytoplankton produce much of the oxygen in our atmosphere.

questions.question: Why is ocean water salty?
options: because it has dissolved minerals from rocks and soil
because people put salt in it

questions.question: What do ocean currents do?
options: they carry warm and cold water around the planet

chapters.title: Making It Home
chapters.text:
Now the family needed to make the shoe feel like home. The old woman and her children worked together to clean and organize their new house. Everyone had a job to do.

The older children swept the floors and washed the windows. The middle children arranged the furniture and put away the dishes. Even the youngest ones helped by putting their toys in the right places.

They hung curtains on the windows and put rugs on the floors. The old woman planted a small garden outside the shoe. Soon their new home looked cozy and welcoming.

That evening, they all sat down together for dinner in their new home. The old woman looked around at her children and smiled. They had turned the giant shoe into a wonderful family home.

vocabulary.definition: to put things in order and arrange them neatly

comprehension.question: Who helped make the shoe into a home?
What did the old woman plant outside?

informationalText.title: Astronauts
informationalText.content:
Astronauts are specially trained people who travel to space to conduct experiments, explore other worlds, and learn more about the universe. They must go through years of difficult training to prepare for the challenges of living and working in space. Astronauts learn how to use complex equipment, work in zero gravity, and handle emergencies that might happen far from Earth.
In space, astronauts live on the International Space Station, where they conduct scientific experiments that help us learn about medicine, materials, and how things behave without gravity. They also maintain the space station and sometimes go on spacewalks to repair equipment or install new parts. The work that astronauts do in space helps improve life on Earth and prepares us for future missions to the Moon and Mars.

questions.question: What do astronauts do in space?
options: they conduct experiments and maintain the space station

chapters.title: Visitors and Friends
chapters.text:
Word spread quickly through the town about the family who lived in a shoe. People were curious and came to see this unusual house. At first, some visitors just stared and pointed.

But when people met the old woman and her children, they saw how kind and friendly the family was. The children were polite and well-behaved. They welcomed visitors with warm smiles.

Soon the neighbors began bringing gifts. Someone brought fresh bread. Another person brought vegetables from their garden. A kind baker brought cookies for all the children.

The old woman was touched by everyone's kindness. "Thank you all," she said. "We may live in a shoe, but we have the best neighbors in the world!" Everyone laughed and agreed.

vocabulary.definition: wanting to know or learn about something
feeling grateful and moved by kindness

comprehension.question: Why did people come to see the shoe house?
options: They were curious about the unusual house
They wanted to complain about it

comprehension.question: How did the neighbors help the family?

informationalText.title: Bicycles
informationalText.content:
Bicycles are vehicles with two wheels that people power by pedaling with their feet. The pedals turn a chain that makes the back wheel spin, moving the bicycle forward. Bicycles have handlebars for steering and brakes to slow down or stop. They are an efficient way to travel because they don't need gasoline or electricity – just human power!
Riding bicycles is good exercise that helps keep people healthy and strong. Bicycles are also good for the environment because they don't produce pollution like cars do. Many cities have bike lanes and paths to make cycling safer and more enjoyable. Some people use bicycles to get to work or school, while others ride them for fun or sport. There are many different types of bicycles designed for different activities, like mountain biking or racing.

questions.question: How do bicycles move forward?
options: pedals turn a chain that makes the back wheel spin

questions.question: Why are bicycles good for the environment?
options: they don't produce pollution like cars do

chapters.title: Happy Ever After
chapters.text:
Months passed, and the shoe house became the happiest home in the whole town. The children had plenty of room to play and learn. They helped each other and shared everything.

The old woman was no longer tired and worried. She had time to read stories to her children, teach them new things, and play games with them. The house was filled with laughter.

Other families in town came to visit and learn from them. They saw how the family worked together and cared for each other. The shoe house became famous for being full of love.

And so the old woman and her twelve children lived happily in their shoe house. They learned that a home is not about how it looks, but about the love and care inside it.

vocabulary.definition: the sound people make when they are happy and laughing

comprehension.question: How did the old woman feel in her new home?
What did the family learn about homes?
options: A home is about love and care, not how it looks

Only fancy houses are good homes

Chapter 1

informationalText.topic: Making do with what you have
informationalText.title: Trains
informationalText.content:
Trains are long vehicles that travel on railroad tracks and can carry many people or large amounts of cargo from one place to another. They are made up of a locomotive that provides the power and several cars that carry passengers or freight. Trains are pulled along steel rails by powerful engines that can run on electricity, diesel fuel, or even steam in older trains.
Trains are an important way to transport people and goods because they can carry much more than trucks or cars. Passenger trains help people travel between cities quickly and comfortably, while freight trains move products like coal, grain, and manufactured goods across long distances. High-speed trains in some countries can travel over 200 miles per hour! Trains are also better for the environment than many other forms of transportation because they can move many people or lots of cargo while using less fuel per person or ton.

questions.question: What pulls trains along the tracks?
Why are trains good for the environment?
options: they can move many people while using less fuel per person
